notes: Dashboard retirement + cleanup pass. (1) Removed the 'What if I invest more?' slider from the Dashboard entirely (markup, the _wireWhatIf handler, and the dashWhatIf wiring). Liquidity health now renders full-width in all cases. The extraMonthly parameter in computeRetirement is left intact (harmless, unused by the UI now) so no engine signature changed. (2) The retirement corpus card is now clickable: tapping it expands an inline detail panel below the hero row. The panel shows a summary table (projected corpus at retirement age, present value in today's money, required corpus, surplus/shortfall, monthly need at retirement) and a year-by-year drawdown table from retirement age to life expectancy (year, age, annual withdrawal inflated at the expense-growth rate, corpus remaining earning the post-retirement return). The drawdown draws from the PROJECTED corpus (the card headline) so the panel and headline always agree. NOTE: this display-only drawdown does NOT match computeRetirement's existing terminalValue/exhaustedAge, which draw only from poolAtRet per each investment's withdraw/SWP/hold strategy; the panel intentionally treats the whole headline corpus as drawable. (3) Added a one-line assumptions note under the card: expense %, inflation rate, drawdown years, and post-retirement return, with a 'tap for details' affordance. Scope is display-only: no projection, net-worth, savings-chain (projectSavingsChain), or computeRetirement math was changed. KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus, so a large current-month expense does not yet re-forecast future savings; that change touches projectSavingsChain and needs dedicated double-count verification. Reusable SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid.
